channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Things to know about the shooting at a Christian school in Wisconsin
item.description: Police in Madison, Wisconsin, are trying to determine what prompted a 15-year-old student to open fire at a private Christian school. The shooting Monday was just days before the winter break. A fellow student and a teacher were killed. The shooter also died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tax brackets and more are changing in 2024, 2025: What to know
item.description: We're more than 100 days out from Tax Day, but there are some impending updates you may want to prepare for.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rear seat belt reminders to be required in 2027
item.description: NHTSA estimated the new rule will prevent more than 500 injuries and save about 50 lives each year if and when it's fully implemented.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Trump sues Des Moines Register, pollster for 'election interference' after pre-election poll
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump sued the Des Moines Register and its pollster for “brazen election interference” in publishing a survey the weekend before the election that showed Democrat Kamala Harris with a surprising lead of three percentage points in the state. The Register's parent Gannett Co.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: College hockey player sued by opponent over injury she suffered on the ice
item.description: A Michigan State University hockey player has been sued by a former opponent for injuries she suffered on the ice.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, ABC 4

item.title: US to pay nearly $116M to settle lawsuits over rampant sexual abuse at California women's prison
item.description: The U.S. government will pay nearly $116 million to resolve lawsuits brought by more than 100 women who say they were abused or mistreated at a now-shuttered federal prison in California that was known as the “rape club” because of rampant staff-on-inmate sexual misconduct.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak And Michael Balsamo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Taco Bell adding chicken nuggets to menus nationwide
item.description: Taco Bell, known for its Mexican-inspired cuisine, will begin serving its take on chicken nuggets later this month.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Shock poll: 41 percent of young voters find killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO acceptable
item.description: Anger over health insurance companies has been in the spotlight after Thompson was fatally shot Dec. 4 in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Event promoters, hotels and lodging sites soon will have to disclose extra fees up front
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission on Tuesday said it will soon require hotels, vacation rental platforms and live event promoters to disclose any fees up front when they list prices.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Connolly bests Ocasio-Cortez in key vote to lead Democrats on Oversight panel
item.description: Rep. Gerry Connolly (D-Va.) on Monday claimed the first round of the two-step contest to lead Democrats on the House Oversight and Accountability Committee in the next Congress, besting Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: An Alabama woman is doing well after the latest experimental pig kidney transplant
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — An Alabama woman is recovering well after a pig kidney transplant last month that freed her from eight years of dialysis, the latest effort to save human lives with animal organs.
item.dc:creator: LAURAN NEERGAARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Government funding bill will run through March, include $100B in disaster aid
item.description: Pieces of a bipartisan effort to prevent a government shutdown during the holiday season are beginning to come into focus, including a March end date and $100 billion in disaster aid. GOP leadership briefed Republicans on Tuesday morning on some of the details of the forthcoming legislation.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: A timeline of the investigation of the Gilgo Beach killings
item.description: For years, women had been disappearing on Long Island. Then in 2010, investigators searching for a missing woman began finding 10 sets of human remains in the scrub along a barrier island parkway, not far from the sands of New York's remote Gilgo Beach.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Aurora police chief: Armed apartment invasion 100% gang activity
item.description: Fourteen people were detained by Aurora police following a reported armed home invasion at the troubled apartment complex, the Edge at Lowry.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, NewsNation

item.title: Trump sues Des Moines Register, pollster
item.description: “Selzer’s polling ‘miss’ was not an astonishing coincidence — it was intentional,” the complaint states.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KRON4

item.title: Boston plans to renovate a crumbling stadium for its new women's soccer team. Not everyone is happy
item.description: Boston is planning to renovate a crumbling stadium in one of the city's most diverse neighborhoods for its new professional women's soccer team.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump tariff plan threatens to drive up gas prices
item.description: Trump said that on his first day in office, he would place 25 percent tariffs on all Canadian and Mexican goods. The two countries are the leading sources of U.S. oil imports.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Democrats divided over Biden administration response to reported drone sightings
item.description: The Biden administration’s response to mounting public concern over alleged drone sightings in the Northeast is dividing Democrats on Capitol Hill, where some lawmakers are defending the executive assurances that there’s no threat to public safety and others are bashing the various agencies for not
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Suspect in Gilgo Beach serial killings is charged in the death of a seventh woman
item.description: The New York architect accused in a string of deaths known as the Gilgo Beach killings has been charged in the death of a seventh woman.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Florida man berated, physically abused child for nearly 30 minutes over missing couch cushion: deputies
item.description: A Florida man is facing child abuse charges after he allegedly screamed obscenities and physically abused a child over a missing couch cushion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Younger Asian Americans navigate something new to their generation: Taking up space
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The chairs stay occupied at 12 Pell. Client after client, they come through the tiny barbershop on a narrow side street in Manhattan’s Chinatown. They come for the cuts, sure. But really, they’re coming for the cool.
item.dc:creator: DEEPTI HAJELA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Drone detectors in New Jersey have found 'little or no evidence' of wrongdoing, governor says
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — Drone-detecting devices deployed in New Jersey in the past week have found “little to no evidence” of anything nefarious or threatening, Gov.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The US Census Bureau is adding refugees to its immigrant count
item.description: The U.S. Census Bureau is changing how it counts immigrants in annual estimates by including more people who were admitted for humanitarian, and often temporary, reasons. The change is being made in an effort to better reflect population shifts this decade, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge sentences Zephen Xaver, man who executed 5 women at Florida bank, to death
item.description: Zephen Xaver pleaded guilty to five counts of murder for killing Ana Piñon Williams, Debra Cook, Marisol Lopez, Cynthia Watson and Jessica Montague at a SunTrust Bank in Sebring, Florida in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, KRON4

item.title: Former FBI informant pleads guilty to fabricating Biden claims
item.description: A former FBI informant on Monday entered a guilty plea on four federal charges related to falsifying statements that in part spurred a GOP congressional investigation into the Biden family.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: California couple on vacation fatally shot in Mexico, officials say
item.description: An American couple from Northern California was shot and killed by gunmen in the western Mexican state of Michoacán, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Create your own custom emojis with iPhone's latest AI update
item.description: Apple has added more AI features to the iPhone with iOS 18.2. With this latest software update, you can now generate AI images and create custom emojis for any conversation, and Siri is getting the power of ChatGPT.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: As schools cut back on bus service, parents turn to rideshare apps
item.description: Reliance on school buses has been waning for years as districts struggle to find drivers and more students attend schools far outside their neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Takeaways from AP's reporting on a child sex abuse crisis inside a secretive Christian sect
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — For decades, a little-known Christian sect has dealt with its abusive ministers and spiritual elders mostly in secret, shunning legal action in favor of urging victims to forgive the predators in their midst.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Godiva steps in to help Metropolitan Ministries during holiday rush
item.description: The iconic pink Godiva truck brought treats for families and volunteers, but their real gift was a $25,000 donation to help restock shelves that have been emptying faster than ever.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers struggle to reach deal to avert government shutdown
item.description: Congressional negotiators are struggling to reach a deal to keep the government's lights on past Dec. 20. Text of a continuing resolution (CR) for a government funding package was expected Sunday ahead of the looming shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: US agencies should use advanced technology to identify mysterious drones, Schumer says
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — After weeks of fear and bewilderment about the drones buzzing over parts of New York and New Jersey, elected officials are urging action to identify and stop the mysterious flights. “There’s a lot of us who are pretty frustrated right now,” Rep. Jim Himes, D-Conn.
item.dc:creator: BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS and DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Private Sin: How a church protected child molesters
item.description: Protestant Reformed Churches in America is struggling with what it has called the "private sin" of child sexual abuse and decades of covering up for abusers, including church leaders and school teachers.
item.dc:creator: Ken Kolker, ABC 4

item.title: Texas company says baby poop and fungi could be the solution to plastic waste
item.description: A dirty diaper may be gross to you, but one Texas company believes it could be the secret ingredient to helping answer an environmental question: How do we clean up all of our plastic waste?
item.dc:creator: Eric Henrikson, KRON4

item.title: Trump leans on extended family for key White House roles
item.description: President-elect Trump’s extended family will fill his inner circle during his second term while his immediate family members step back from formal roles, a change in dynamics from his first administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WGN 9

item.title: Spotify too repetitive? Here's how to retrain the algorithm
item.description: Over the past couple years, complaints have piled up in online forums from music lovers having trouble discovering new music. Why? No matter what they do, Spotify seems to be serving them the same old songs, again and again.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Thousands of Santas and a few Grinches hit the streets for annual SantaCon bar crawls
item.description: Thousands of Kris Kringles and jolly Old St. Nicks are jamming streets, sidewalks and bars from Adelaide to Vancouver on Saturday for annual SantaCon charity pub crawls.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
